g.text: A hybrid image encryption system that combines seeded
pixel permutation with matrix-based transformations to
deliver highly secure, lossless, and key-dependent
The encryption process starts by converting the
image into a pixel matrix. Each pixel's position and
color intensity are recorded numerically. A key-
based random generator then shuffles all pixel
positions, breaking the natural arrangement so the
image becomes unrecognizable — the first secure
layer of encryption. Only the correct key can
reproduce the same shuffle order for decryption.
cation and modular transforms using key-derived
matrices — add diffusion, spreading small changes
widely. Even minor key variations produce entirely
different encrypted images. During decryption, the
matrix operations are reversed first, then pixels are
restored to their original order using the same key —
yielding the exact original image with zero loss.
Even a slight change in key produces
